text:
Community Living Guide
Living in a UCF residence hall or apartment gives you the opportunity to get involved in a community and enjoy many exciting experiences. The on-campus community helps you form lifelong friendships and lasting memories.
The Housing and Residence Life staff is here to assist you, but we recommend that you take the lead by participating, listening to others, sharing your experiences and knowledge, adhering to the community's expectations, and being a role model for others.
Learn more about how you can make the most of your on-campus living experience by reading the Communi

model for others.
Learn more about how you can make the most of your on-campus living experience by reading the Community Living Guide.
The full Community Living Guide (CLG) is published as a PDF and is updated periodically. The current edition referenced from this page is CLG-4_16_26.pdf (April 16, 2026 revision):
https://www.housing.ucf.edu/wp-content/uploads/sites/97/2026/04/CLG-4_16_26.pdf
NOTE: The detailed rules, expectations, and behaviors that all residents must follow live in the CLG PDF itself (saved separately in this corpus as 02_community_living_guide_full.txt).

WELCOME HOME, UCF KNIGHTS
Living in a UCF residence hall or apartment gives you the opportunity to get involved in a community and enjoy many exciting experiences. The Housing and Residence Life staff is here to assist you, but recommends residents take the lead by participating, listening, sharing knowledge, adhering to community expectations, and being a role model.
MISSION: To provide residents with safe, engaging housing communities that foster student success through innovative living and learning opportunities.
VISION: To be the premier experience for residents to live, learn, and become

ovative living and learning opportunities.
VISION: To be the premier experience for residents to live, learn, and become tomorrow's citizens.
THE UCF CREED: Integrity, Scholarship, Community, Creativity, Excellence.
DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND RESIDENCE LIFE (DHRL)
DHRL is comprised of 450+ student staff and full-time employees serving a residential population of approximately 7,500 students across 12 communities. Staffed mail centers exist on-campus (Apollo/Libra, Academic Village, Lake Claire, West Plaza) and off-campus (Rosen, Northview, UnionWest). The Housing Lockshop programs Persona cards

lage, Lake Claire, West Plaza) and off-campus (Rosen, Northview, UnionWest). The Housing Lockshop programs Persona cards/student IDs for room access and is located in the Main Campus Housing Services Building, Neptune 159, (407) 823-1239.
Staff roles include: Resident Assistant (RA) — community facilitator who answers questions, plans programs, and enforces policy; Office Assistant (OA) and Office Support Assistant (OSA) — front-desk operations, lockouts, work orders; Graduate Coordinator; Coordinator and Assistant Director, Residence Life and Education.
PARKING (residential)
Main campus parki

e Coordinator; Coordinator and Assistant Director, Residence Life and Education.
PARKING (residential)
Main campus parking for residential students is managed by UCF Parking Services (https://parking.ucf.edu/). All motorized vehicles must be registered online at parking.ucf.edu before picking up a decal at UCF Parking Services in Garage B. Parking differs by community. Northview residents must contact the Northview Office about a parking pass; improper parking at Northview is subject to a $30 fine.
HEALTH, SAFETY AND ACCESSIBILITY
The safety of students is a top priority; staff is available 24

subject to a $30 fine.
HEALTH, SAFETY AND ACCESSIBILITY
The safety of students is a top priority; staff is available 24 hours a day. Residents are expected to report unacceptable behavior to Housing & Residence Life staff and/or the UCF or Orlando Police Department. Once a person is reported for an alleged crime or policy violation, anonymity cannot be guaranteed, as all allegations are confronted and the accused resident has the right to full disclosure.
Safety features include: staffed desks throughout day/evening; strategically placed video cameras; fire and life-safety equipment; schedule

lude: staffed desks throughout day/evening; strategically placed video cameras; fire and life-safety equipment; scheduled staff rounds after hours, weekends, and holidays; 24-hour staff access; and the visitation/guest policy.
Safety tips: Never walk alone at night and don't let friends walk alone. Avoid dimly lit or unlit areas. Never prop open outside doors. Report unescorted or unfamiliar persons to staff immediately. Use the blue-light emergency phones. Call 911 for any emergency. Always lock your door and secure valuables. "See Something, Say Something."
UCF POLICE DEPARTMENT (UCFPD): Est

mergency. Always lock your door and secure valuables. "See Something, Say Something."
UCF POLICE DEPARTMENT (UCFPD): Established 1973; ~75 sworn officers serving over 67,000 students and 11,000 employees. Non-emergency: (407) 823-5555. Emergency: 911.
FIRE SAFETY & COOKING: Residents must abide by state and federal fire safety laws; violations can lead to criminal prosecution and removal from housing. Cooking guidance: turn on the vent above the stove, never leave food unattended, check the oven before use, turn off appliances when done, follow microwave/food-prep instructions. If a smoke dete

nded, check the oven before use, turn off appliances when done, follow microwave/food-prep instructions. If a smoke detector triggers with no fire: call the community office, identify yourself/location, turn on vent/fan and open windows, do not prop the apartment door (smoke may set off the building alarm and result in a charge).
SMOKE-FREE CAMPUS: UCF is smoke-free for faculty, staff, students, and visitors, including medicinal products.
COMMUNITY LIVING GUIDE REGULATIONS (summary of numbered policies)
Residents found in violation may face educational or punitive sanctioning, including admini

ummary of numbered policies)
Residents found in violation may face educational or punitive sanctioning, including administrative cancellation of the housing contract. DHRL may change or add policies during the year.
1. ALCOHOL — Underage (under 21) possession/consumption prohibited; manufacturing/selling prohibited; providing alcohol to under-21 prohibited; possession of alcohol in a shared space with anyone under 21 prohibited (incl. empty containers); common-source containers (kegs, party balls) prohibited; alcohol paraphernalia for overconsumption prohibited; 21+ residents may not consume i

rs (kegs, party balls) prohibited; alcohol paraphernalia for overconsumption prohibited; 21+ residents may not consume in the presence of those under 21. Staff will ask violators to dispose of items; anyone present in a space containing alcohol may be found in violation.
2. ANIMALS & PETS — All animals except fish are prohibited. Aggressive/venomous fish prohibited. One fish tank under 10 gallons per resident. Feeding/touching/harming campus wildlife prohibited. Service/Emotional Support Animals require prior approval from Student Accessibility Services (SAS) and registration with Housing. Vio

onal Support Animals require prior approval from Student Accessibility Services (SAS) and registration with Housing. Violations result in contract cancellation and referral to the CLG process.
3. BEDS — Residents may not raise, loft, or alter beds (no concrete blocks, risers, platforms). Communities with adjustable Twin XL beds: Hercules, Nike, Neptune, Apollo, Libra, Lake Claire, Rosen. Northview and Towers do NOT have adjustable beds. Labor charges apply for reassembly of altered beds.
4. BICYCLES — Bicycles may not be stored inside residential communities and may only be locked to universit

ltered beds.
4. BICYCLES — Bicycles may not be stored inside residential communities and may only be locked to university-approved bike racks. Register bikes at https://police.ucf.edu/property-registration.
5. COMPUTER USAGE — Personal wireless connections (routers, tethering, splitters) prohibited. All technology must be registered with UCF IT for UCF wifi.
6. COMMUNITY DISRUPTION — Noise interfering with study/sleep prohibited (pounding, amplified sound, instruments, subwoofer bass). Quiet hours: 10:00 p.m.–8:00 a.m. during the academic year, and 24 hours during finals. Noxious odors (cigare

fer bass). Quiet hours: 10:00 p.m.–8:00 a.m. during the academic year, and 24 hours during finals. Noxious odors (cigarette, marijuana, perfume, air freshener, dirty laundry, etc.) prohibited. Tampering with hall resources prohibited.
7. CONTROLLED SUBSTANCES — Illegal drugs and controlled substances prohibited; medical marijuana is NOT allowed in UCF owned/managed/affiliated halls; misused prescription/non-prescription drugs prohibited; drug paraphernalia prohibited; being in the presence of drugs/paraphernalia prohibited. Violations may result in agreement termination; students pay terminati

n the presence of drugs/paraphernalia prohibited. Violations may result in agreement termination; students pay termination fees plus the remaining term's rent and must vacate within 72 hours.
8. DISRUPTIVE CONDUCT — Behavior adversely impacting residents/communities prohibited.
9. ELECTRICAL & APPLIANCES — Unapproved electrical devices prohibited; octopus/torchiere/halogen lamps prohibited; exposed-element appliances prohibited outside kitchens; appliances over 1,000 watts prohibited; refrigerators over 5 cubic feet prohibited; scented plug-ins prohibited; 3-D printers prohibited; induction de

ohibited; refrigerators over 5 cubic feet prohibited; scented plug-ins prohibited; 3-D printers prohibited; induction devices prohibited. Surge protectors/extension cords must be UL-grounded three-prong with internal circuit breaker, max six outlets, no damage, load under 20 amps, not under carpet/furniture. LED strip lights with adhesive backs not permitted. String lights allowed around windows only, away from flammables.
10. FAILURE TO COMPLY — Non-compliance with staff/official instructions prohibited.
11. FAILURE TO REPORT — Failing to report CLG/policy violations is prohibited; students p

icial instructions prohibited.
11. FAILURE TO REPORT — Failing to report CLG/policy violations is prohibited; students present during a violation may be held responsible.
12. FIRE SAFETY — No starting fires, false alarms, or tampering with safety equipment; must evacuate during alarms (Florida Statute 633.202); no explosives, flammables, open flames (candles/incense), live trees, space heaters; no splicing electrical wiring; wall decorations may not cover more than 20% of a wall; no curtains as partitions; nothing affixed to ceilings that could cause fire or block sprinklers. Closet curtains v

all; no curtains as partitions; nothing affixed to ceilings that could cause fire or block sprinklers. Closet curtains via tension rod must sit 20 inches below the ceiling for sprinkler coverage.
13. GAMBLING — Prohibited in all residential communities.
14. GAMES & SPORTS — Physical recreation indoors (scooters, soccer, football, skateboarding, rollerblading, basketball) prohibited; activities threatening property prohibited; fishing prohibited on UCF property.
15. HAZING — Prohibited.
16. HARMFUL BEHAVIOR — Physical harm, threats, harassment, bullying, verbal/written abuse, or retaliation pro

rohibited.
16. HARMFUL BEHAVIOR — Physical harm, threats, harassment, bullying, verbal/written abuse, or retaliation prohibited.
17. HOUSEKEEPING — Must maintain a clean space; remove trash weekly; store open food in containers; no trash/personal items in shared areas; cannot refuse custodial/maintenance services. Fees apply for cleaning/facility issues; annual communities may incur "Welcome Ready" fines.
18. IDENTIFICATION — Must present valid UCF/government ID on request; no lending/borrowing IDs; no false ID info; persons without ID treated as trespassing.
19. ILLEGAL ENTRY & TRESPASSING —

o lending/borrowing IDs; no false ID info; persons without ID treated as trespassing.
19. ILLEGAL ENTRY & TRESPASSING — No unauthorized entry to living/restricted areas; no admitting unknown persons; no propping/disabling locks; no entry when communities are closed; no roof access.
20. KEYS — No unauthorized use/duplication; no switching/borrowing; report lost keys immediately; return mail keys; only hand keys to DHRL staff at checkout.
21. PUBLIC AREAS — Common areas/corridors must stay clear of obstructions, personal property, and trash.
22. RECORDING OTHERS — Recording/distributing another'

rs must stay clear of obstructions, personal property, and trash.
22. RECORDING OTHERS — Recording/distributing another's image or voice without permission where privacy is expected is prohibited.
23. ROOMMATE AGREEMENT — Violating the roommate agreement is prohibited; revisit changes with your RA.
24. ROOM CHANGES — No changing assignments/moving without preapproval from the community Coordinator/office; follow the room-change process; unauthorized changes risk conduct violations.
25. ROOM USE PROHIBITIONS — No permanent/semi-permanent alterations (painting, removing/stacking furniture); no s

tions.
25. ROOM USE PROHIBITIONS — No permanent/semi-permanent alterations (painting, removing/stacking furniture); no subletting; no on-site businesses; no use of vacant spaces/furniture; no relocating common-area furniture; no mounting items to walls/furniture; no decorating common areas without approval; no shower caddies hung from shower heads. Charges assessed to return space to move-in-ready condition; a vacancy may be filled by a new roommate at any time.
26. SEXUAL MISCONDUCT — All forms prohibited; subject to law enforcement, conduct violations, and agreement termination under Title I

SCONDUCT — All forms prohibited; subject to law enforcement, conduct violations, and agreement termination under Title IX. Sexual activity/masturbation in the presence of others is a CLG violation even if not a Title IX violation.
27. SMOKING — All smoking, including e-cigarettes/juuls/inhalant pens, prohibited inside hall entryways; UCF is smoke-free (https://smokefree.sswb.ucf.edu/), including medicinal products.
28. SOLICITATION — Soliciting and unapproved flyers/postings/chalk art prohibited.
29. TRANSPORTATION — Unauthorized motorized vehicles outside designated zones prohibited; no vehic

chalk art prohibited.
29. TRANSPORTATION — Unauthorized motorized vehicles outside designated zones prohibited; no vehicle maintenance in communities; non-motorized transport (skateboards, scooters, etc.) may not be used in facilities; hoverboards/boosted boards/self-balancing scooters may not be charged/stored/used in or around UCF communities. See https://parking.ucf.edu/electric-scooters/.
30. VANDALISM & THEFT — Willful destruction, defacement, or theft of property prohibited.
31. VISITATION & GUESTS —
A. Unescorted guests prohibited.
B. Overnight guests for more than three consecutive nig

ited.
31. VISITATION & GUESTS —
A. Unescorted guests prohibited.
B. Overnight guests for more than three consecutive nights in a semester prohibited (overnight = any guest past midnight).
C. Overnight guests for more than seven nights total in a semester prohibited.
D. Each resident may have up to two guests at a time (e.g., a triple has a max occupancy of nine: three residents + six guests).
E. Must give roommates adequate notice of overnight guests — 48 hours unless the Roommate Agreement says otherwise.
F. Cohabitation by anyone not assigned to the room prohibited.
G. Gatherings exceeding d

mmate Agreement says otherwise.
F. Cohabitation by anyone not assigned to the room prohibited.
G. Gatherings exceeding designated room/apartment occupancy prohibited.
H. Residents are personally and financially responsible for their guests' behavior and policy violations. A roommate's rights to privacy, sleep, and study take precedence over a host's right to have a guest.
32. WEAPONS — Possession/storage/manufacture/use of weapons or munitions prohibited; using items to cause fear/harm prohibited; kitchen knives only for cooking in kitchens (chef's knives kept in a soft carrier/block); no ship

fear/harm prohibited; kitchen knives only for cooking in kitchens (chef's knives kept in a soft carrier/block); no shipping weapons to a resident PO Box. Prohibited weapons include guns (BB/pellet, nerf, water, airsoft, paintball), sporting/hunting, edged (knives, swords), blunt (martial arts, nightstick), pocket knives over 2 inches, tasers, stun guns. A self-defense chemical spray up to 2 ounces is allowed.
33. WINDOWS & SCREENS — No obscuring/covering/blocking windows; nothing thrown/hung from windows; windows not used as entry/exit except in emergencies; no tampering with/removing screens

thing thrown/hung from windows; windows not used as entry/exit except in emergencies; no tampering with/removing screens or latches. Windows must remain closed and locked except in an emergency or to ventilate smoke.
STUDENT SUPPORT
Student Care Services (SCS) supports students facing academic, financial, or personal challenges and partners with Housing: https://scs.sswb.ucf.edu/
Title IX: UCF prohibits sex discrimination, sexual harassment, and sexual violence; report to the Title IX Coordinator or any Housing staff. Resources: https://letsbeclear.ucf.edu/
CONDUCT PROCESS (CLG flowchart, summ

Title IX Coordinator or any Housing staff. Resources: https://letsbeclear.ucf.edu/
CONDUCT PROCESS (CLG flowchart, summarized): An alleged violation generates an Incident Report (IR) → reviewed and assigned to a hearing officer → hearing officer determines whether a conduct meeting is needed → a CLG Violation Meeting is scheduled and a letter is sent → the hearing officer meets with those involved (a decision can be made without a student's input if they do not appear) → if found responsible, a warning letter may be issued (no response required) and the case is closed; other outcomes follow t

found responsible, a warning letter may be issued (no response required) and the case is closed; other outcomes follow the appeals process described in the CLG.
[Document continues in the source PDF with detailed sections on rights/facilities, NorthView guidelines, the appeals process, and contact information. For the complete official text, see the PDF URL above.]

Housing Options
UCF offers traditional suite-style and apartment-style housing on campus. All UCF Housing communities come with 24-hour staff support, furnished rooms, all utilities included, high-speed Wi-Fi, individual agreements, and central locations.
AGREEMENT TYPES AND COMMUNITIES
Academic-Year Agreement
A 2-semester agreement (Fall and Spring only), available for first-year students. Residents depart for winter break between semesters. Communities include a community center, laundry rooms, common areas, and access to a community kitchen:
- Apollo
- Nike and Hercules
- Lake Claire
- Libr

enter, laundry rooms, common areas, and access to a community kitchen:
- Apollo
- Nike and Hercules
- Lake Claire
- Libra
- Neptune
Annual Agreements
A 3-semester agreement (Fall, Spring, and the following Summer), available for first-year students and upperclassmen. Residents may remain in their rooms over semester breaks. These communities are apartment-style with private bedrooms, kitchens, and furnished living areas:
- Towers at Knights Plaza
- NorthView
Rosen Agreement
Includes Fall, Spring, and the option to add the following Summer — the only hybrid agreement. Available for first-year s

ncludes Fall, Spring, and the option to add the following Summer — the only hybrid agreement. Available for first-year students and upperclassmen. Apartment-style rooms with full kitchens, living rooms, and a private community pool. The Rosen Campus is in Orlando's Entertainment District, about 30 minutes from main campus:
- Rosen College Apartments
OFF-CAMPUS RESOURCES
- Office of Student Neighborhood Relations (guidance/support for off-campus searches): https://nrse.sswb.ucf.edu/
- UCF Off-Campus Partners (listings): https://ucf.offcampuspartners.com/
AFFILIATED OFF-CAMPUS HOUSING
Affiliated

f.edu/
- UCF Off-Campus Partners (listings): https://ucf.offcampuspartners.com/
AFFILIATED OFF-CAMPUS HOUSING
Affiliated properties are independently managed but affiliated with UCF. There are three:
- Knights Circle (near Main Campus): https://www.liveatknightscircle.com/
- The Pointe at Central (near Main Campus): https://www.thepointeatcentral.com/
- UnionWest Student Living (UCF Downtown Orlando Campus): https://unionweststudentliving.com/
CONFERENCE / INTERN HOUSING
Summer conference housing is available for students with Orlando-area internships and for organizations hosting overnight ca
er conference housing is available for students with Orlando-area internships and for organizations hosting overnight camps/conferences. See Conference Housing and Non-UCF Scholars/Interns pages.

Eligibility for UCF Housing
Eligibility for UCF Housing is based on your student classification and the semester(s) for which you are seeking housing. Eligibility for a particular type of housing does not guarantee availability; availability varies each year. Students may apply for UCF Housing once they are offered admission, and are encouraged to apply as early as possible. If you cannot apply for a type of housing you are eligible for, you may join a waitlist.
Married and family housing is not available at UCF. Students in the UCF Online degree program are not eligible to live in UCF Housing

amily housing is not available at UCF. Students in the UCF Online degree program are not eligible to live in UCF Housing.
ELIGIBILITY BY CLASSIFICATION (summary)
- First-Year Students: Given preference for on-campus housing on the Orlando main campus. Academic-Year private rooms are very limited for Fall/Spring; some options are based on availability for Spring-only and Spring/Summer.
- Current Residents: May apply for Towers and NorthView (Annual, Fall/Spring/Summer) via lottery; Rosen Spring-only is "contact us."
- Off-Campus, Graduate, and Transfer Students: Most live off-campus. On-campus

tery; Rosen Spring-only is "contact us."
- Off-Campus, Graduate, and Transfer Students: Most live off-campus. On-campus options are limited and primarily NorthView (lottery) and Rosen College Apartments (first-come, first-served).
CHOOSING AN AGREEMENT
All residence halls share amenities: high-speed Wi-Fi, laundry, included utilities (no caps), maintenance, and social programs.
- Academic Agreement = TWO semesters (Fall + Spring); residents must vacate during winter break. Communities: Apollo, Libra, Nike & Hercules, Neptune, Lake Claire.
- Annual Agreement = THREE semesters (Fall, Spring, fol

mmunities: Apollo, Libra, Nike & Hercules, Neptune, Lake Claire.
- Annual Agreement = THREE semesters (Fall, Spring, following Summer); residents may stay over winter break but must commit to the full duration. Communities: Towers, NorthView, Rosen (Rosen can add a summer semester).
- Most centrally located halls: Libra and Apollo (Academic Agreement).
- Private bedrooms: Towers, NorthView, Rosen (all apartment-style).
- All-freshmen communities: Apollo, Libra, Lake Claire, Neptune, Nike & Hercules.
LIVING LEARNING COMMUNITIES (LLCs)
For first-year students to connect to a smaller community. C

eptune, Nike & Hercules.
LIVING LEARNING COMMUNITIES (LLCs)
For first-year students to connect to a smaller community. Current LLCs: EXCEL/COMPASS, Nursing @ Nike, Honors. All LLCs except Honors are in Academic Agreement communities. Honors LLC is in Tower III and requires a Towers Agreement; Honors students are NOT given priority for on-campus housing.
TRANSFER / GRADUATE / UPPERCLASSMEN
On-campus housing is very limited. UCF recommends off-campus options. Resources: the Office of Student Neighborhood Relations (https://nrse.sswb.ucf.edu/), the Knight's Guide to Living Off Campus, and the UCF

ice of Student Neighborhood Relations (https://nrse.sswb.ucf.edu/), the Knight's Guide to Living Off Campus, and the UCF Off-Campus Partners website (https://ucf.offcampuspartners.com/). Towers and NorthView use a lottery for returning residents, upperclassmen, and off-campus students; Rosen is first-come, first-served. Apply and submit prepayment during the lottery/application window.
RATES: Rental rates are listed by semester, per person, per room type, approved by the UCF Board of Trustees each year and subject to change (https://www.housing.ucf.edu/rates/).
SUMMER B: First-year students ad

Board of Trustees each year and subject to change (https://www.housing.ucf.edu/rates/).
SUMMER B: First-year students admitted for Summer B must apply for a separate summer-only agreement in addition to their Fall agreement.

How to Apply for UCF Housing
UCF Housing offers shared suite and apartment-style floorplans. All communities include 24-hour staff support, furnished rooms, all utilities (no caps), Wi-Fi, individual leases, and central locations. First-year students are given preference for housing on the Orlando main campus. Students of all classifications may apply for housing at Rosen College Apartments, NorthView, or an affiliated option.
STEPS
1. Choose your campus and housing agreement. (First-year students admitted for Summer B must apply for a separate summer-only agreement in addition to their Fall a

. (First-year students admitted for Summer B must apply for a separate summer-only agreement in addition to their Fall agreement. The Rosen agreement includes Fall and Spring with the option to add Summer.)
2. Apply for housing online: Log in to myUCF > Student Self Service > Housing > Housing Portal. Select semester, campus, and agreement type; create your profile; sign the terms and conditions; submit your prepayment to complete the application.
3. Apply for a Living Learning Community (optional) — a group of students placed together by common interest or academic program.
4. Verify your agr

ng Community (optional) — a group of students placed together by common interest or academic program.
4. Verify your agreement status. Status changes from "submitted" to "confirmed" once space is verified; you'll receive an email and can view status in the Housing Portal.
5. Find roommates (roommate matching).
6. Online Room Selection — select your room with your roommate group from May through July; appointment times are based on when you completed your application; rooms are first-applied, first-served.
7. Move in.
KEY NOTES
- No student is guaranteed on-campus housing; apply early.
- A prep

e first-applied, first-served.
7. Move in.
KEY NOTES
- No student is guaranteed on-campus housing; apply early.
- A prepayment is required for the agreement to be accepted/confirmed.
- Confirmed students who skip online room selection are assigned by staff to remaining rooms.
- Housing agreements may be cancelled and prepayments forfeited if the admissions enrollment deposit is not paid.
GENERAL HOUSING AGREEMENT TIMELINES
Incoming Freshmen:
- Fall: Agreements open mid-November, close when full, confirm/waitlist on a rolling basis, room selection in June.
- Summer B: Open mid-November, close w

er, close when full, confirm/waitlist on a rolling basis, room selection in June.
- Summer B: Open mid-November, close when full, rolling confirmation, room selection in May.
- Spring: Open early November, close late November, confirm early December, room selection mid-December.
Current Residents / Transfer / Graduate / Off-Campus Students:
- Fall Lottery (Main): Open early January, close late January, confirm/waitlist late January, room selection February/March.
- Fall Rosen: Open early January, close when full, rolling confirmation, room selection March/April.
- Summer A/B/C (Main): Open mid

: Open early January, close when full, rolling confirmation, room selection March/April.
- Summer A/B/C (Main): Open mid-February, close mid-March, confirm early April, room selection April.
- Summer A/B/C (Rosen): Open mid-February, close when full, rolling confirmation, room selection April.
- Spring: Open early November, close late November, confirm early December, room selection mid-December.

Safety in UCF Housing
UCF Housing provides a safe environment for students living on their own for the first time. Each residence hall has controlled building access and secure entry points and is staffed 24 hours a day by trained Residence Life professionals, with campus police offering continuous security and support. Staff are often among the first to respond to student concerns.
ON-CAMPUS SECURITY — SAFETY STEPS
- Never walk alone at night and do not let friends walk alone.
- Avoid dimly lit or unlit areas on campus.
- Never prop open outside doors of your residence hall.
- Report unescort

e.
- Avoid dimly lit or unlit areas on campus.
- Never prop open outside doors of your residence hall.
- Report unescorted or unfamiliar persons to staff immediately.
- Be familiar with the blue-light emergency phones and use them.
- Call 407-823-5555 (UCFPD non-emergency) or 911 for any emergency.
- Always lock the door to your residence hall room.
- Inform your RA of any crisis, theft, or emergency.
- Report anything out of the ordinary to your community office.
Safety resources: Resident Fire Safety (ehs.ucf.edu/fire-safety-guidelines), UCF Police Department (police.ucf.edu), UCF Environmen

urces: Resident Fire Safety (ehs.ucf.edu/fire-safety-guidelines), UCF Police Department (police.ucf.edu), UCF Environmental Health & Safety (ehs.ucf.edu), UCF Emergency Management, UCF Facilities & Safety, UCF Victim Services (victimservices.ucf.edu).
KEYS AND LOCKOUTS
- You receive your room key at check-in and are responsible for it the entire academic year; keep it with you at all times.
- If you lose your key, a new lock core and keys are ordered and your account is billed for the new lock core.
- If locked out, bring your UCF ID to your community office for assistance.
- Residence hall st

d for the new lock core.
- If locked out, bring your UCF ID to your community office for assistance.
- Residence hall staff will NOT open another resident's room for you at any time.
BIKES
Bikes are not permitted inside residence halls. Register bikes with UCF Parking Services and obtain a permit for campus use. Bike racks/storage are available in all communities; lock bikes at all times to prevent theft.
RENTERS INSURANCE
UCF strongly encourages renter's insurance. UCF and DHRL are NOT responsible for damage or loss of belongings from events such as fire, sprinkler discharge, or hurricanes (s

HRL are NOT responsible for damage or loss of belongings from events such as fire, sprinkler discharge, or hurricanes (see Section 11: Limit of Liability in the housing agreement). Students should secure their own personal/property loss insurance.
HURRICANE PREP AND EVACUATION
Monitor emergency updates on the UCF website and follow Housing staff instructions. Students who can safely return home or relocate off-campus should do so before a storm. All residents must complete the Weather Emergency Plan form in the Housing Portal (myUCF > Student Self Service > Housing > Housing Portal). If Ride-O

e Weather Emergency Plan form in the Housing Portal (myUCF > Student Self Service > Housing > Housing Portal). If Ride-Out locations are designated, students must leave their residence and report to the assigned location (UCF ID required for check-in).
- What to bring: clothing/bedding (sleeping bag or blankets), toiletries and prescription medications, important documents, non-perishable food for at least three days, flashlight/batteries/portable charger, weather radio, personal items for entertainment.
- Not allowed: pets; alcohol, tobacco, or controlled substances; weapons.
Contact your RA

onal items for entertainment.
- Not allowed: pets; alcohol, tobacco, or controlled substances; weapons.
Contact your RA or community office with questions.

Open Housing
UCF Housing and Residence Life offers an Open Housing option to students of all classifications and in all housing agreement types. Open Housing allows students to reside together in the same room, suite, or apartment, regardless of sex. When students complete a housing application, they are given the opportunity to opt in to Open Housing. Students who do not opt in will roommate match and room select with students who have the same sex listed on their myUCF.
OPEN HOUSING GUIDELINES
- Any student may opt in to Open Housing when completing a housing agreement.
- Students opting in

N HOUSING GUIDELINES
- Any student may opt in to Open Housing when completing a housing agreement.
- Students opting in can only roommate match with other students of the same housing agreement type who have also opted in.
- Students will not be automatically placed in an Open Housing suite/apartment unless they indicated on their agreement that they'd be comfortable living in Open Housing.
- Students must be able to fill all spaces in the suite/apartment with other students who requested Open Housing during room selection.
- Open Housing is the student's decision and is never forced on an ind

who requested Open Housing during room selection.
- Open Housing is the student's decision and is never forced on an individual. Vacancies are filled by another individual opting in to Open Housing if possible.
- UCF DHRL may initiate consolidation procedures to prevent vacant Open Housing spaces; affected students are notified by email.
- UCF DHRL encourages students to discuss the program with parents/guardians beforehand.
- The program is NOT designed for romantic couples; students in intimate relationships should not choose to live together in Open Housing.
- Requesting Open Housing does n

students in intimate relationships should not choose to live together in Open Housing.
- Requesting Open Housing does not guarantee a specific room type or building; room selection is based on availability.
- Students who opted in may opt out during the Roommate Matching timeframe in the Housing Portal.

source_type: Official UCF page (+ official UCF Dining membership info)
text:
What are my dining options?
UCF Dining Memberships (meal plans) are convenient and flexible. Anyone with a valid UCF ID may purchase a Dining Membership. All members gain access to dining locations across campus. All Dining Memberships are voluntary — there is no mandatory meal plan requirement — providing an open-access community atmosphere to study, socialize, and eat. Members get value whether they eat on campus three times a week or three times a day.
If you don't want a membership, there is a variety of dining establishments to choose from, including Chick-fil-A, Knightro's, Einstein Bros

source_type: Official UCF page (+ official UCF Dining membership info)
text:
membership, there is a variety of dining establishments to choose from, including Chick-fil-A, Knightro's, Einstein Bros. Bagels, Huey Magoo's, Gringos Locos, Starbucks, and many others. Dining Services website: https://ucf.campusdish.com/ (also https://ucf.mydininghub.com/en)
HOW MEAL PLANS WORK
A Meal Plan includes Meal Swipes and Dining Dollars, loaded onto your UCF student ID (physical, virtual, or digital meal pass).
- Meal Swipes give access to the all-you-care-to-eat dining halls: '63 South and Knightro's.
- Dining Dollars are prepaid funds usable at any UCF Dining location across campu

source_type: Official UCF page (+ official UCF Dining membership info)
text:
ining halls: '63 South and Knightro's.
- Dining Dollars are prepaid funds usable at any UCF Dining location across campus.
- A Meal Plan unlocks dining at locations across campus (about 25 locations cited by UCF Dining).
- Guest passes can be purchased when entering the dining halls ('63 South and Knightro's) with Dining Dollars, credit card, or cash.
MEMBERSHIP TYPES (per UCF Dining Meal Plan Terms & Conditions)
- ANY 10: Any active UCF student. Ten meals per week at '63 South and Knightro's (8-month and 4-month memberships). Meals reset every Monday; unused swipes do not carry over.
- ANY 14

source_type: Official UCF page (+ official UCF Dining membership info)
text:
th and Knightro's (8-month and 4-month memberships). Meals reset every Monday; unused swipes do not carry over.
- ANY 14: Any active UCF student. Fourteen meals per week at '63 South and Knightro's. Meals reset every Monday; unused swipes do not carry over.
- "Create Your Own" memberships (for Lake Claire, Towers, or off-campus residents; resident status verified):
- KNIGHTS 25: 25 meals per semester at '63 South and Knightro's.
- KNIGHTS 50: 50 meals per semester; choose 400, 500, or 600 Dining Dollars per semester.
- KNIGHTS 80: 80 meals per semester; choose 400, 500, or 600 Dining Dollars p

source_type: Official UCF page (+ official UCF Dining membership info)
text:
, 500, or 600 Dining Dollars per semester.
- KNIGHTS 80: 80 meals per semester; choose 400, 500, or 600 Dining Dollars per semester.
- Stand-alone Dining Dollars plans are also offered (e.g., 775 Dining Dollars).
CHECKING BALANCES
Check Meal Swipes and Dining Dollars on a purchase receipt, at the meal plan office in Ferrell Commons (Mon–Fri 9 a.m.–5 p.m.), or by linking your UCF ID to your CampusDish/dining hub account online.

Transportation Options & Parking — UCF Downtown
UCF Downtown is a transit-oriented campus offering many ways to get to and from campus: driving and carpools, express campus shuttles, public transit (SunRail, Lynx, Lymmo), and biking.
EXPRESS SHUTTLES
UCF Parking and Transportation Services runs 15 daily roundtrip express shuttles between the UCF main campus' Lynx Transit Center (between Garage A and Garage I) and UCF Downtown, open to UCF students and employees with a valid UCF ID. Hours: Monday–Thursday 6:30 a.m.–10:30 p.m. and Friday 6:30 a.m.–8:30 p.m., except holidays/closures. All shuttle

CF ID. Hours: Monday–Thursday 6:30 a.m.–10:30 p.m. and Friday 6:30 a.m.–8:30 p.m., except holidays/closures. All shuttles have WiFi and are ADA compliant.
GROCERY SHUTTLE
Available to UCF and Valencia students from the downtown campus to the Publix at The Paramount on Lake Eola (400 E. Central Blvd.). Typically runs twice per month on Tuesdays with two roundtrips (departing campus 12:30 p.m. and 8:30 p.m.; departing Publix 1:45 p.m. and 9:45 p.m.). Pick-up/drop-off along Livingston Street in front of Dr. Phillips Academic Commons (528 W. Livingston Street). A valid UCF or Valencia Downtown ID

ngston Street in front of Dr. Phillips Academic Commons (528 W. Livingston Street). A valid UCF or Valencia Downtown ID is required.
PARKING
There are about 2,000 parking spaces supporting the downtown campus, including dedicated permit parking for students/faculty/staff, on-street and metered visitor spaces, and designated parking for UnionWest residents/visitors. Parking permits are required to park in UCF lots and garages, designated by permit type. There is NO overnight parking on the downtown campus or in the City garages at Creative Village.
- Students (Commuter): Commuting students purc

arking on the downtown campus or in the City garages at Creative Village.
- Students (Commuter): Commuting students purchase a Student "D" permit, allowing parking in all General (green) lots and unreserved garages. After 5:30 p.m., the permit also allows parking in Employee spaces unless noted.
- UnionWest Residential Students: The UnionWest garage is the only overnight parking option on-campus; each resident can lease one assigned space in the attached garage.
- Faculty/Staff: Employee permits allow parking in all designated Employee or General lots and unreserved garages.
- Guest/Visitor: V

ff: Employee permits allow parking in all designated Employee or General lots and unreserved garages.
- Guest/Visitor: Visitors may purchase an $8 daily virtual parking permit. The first level of the Parramore Garage is daily virtual permit parking only; overflow on the 4th floor of Parramore or any level of the Amelia Garage. Daily permits are valid in "D" (green) spaces in Parramore or Amelia; after 5:30 p.m., valid in red or blue spaces. Metered on-street parking on Livingston Street (and an hourly lot near the Marriott) is managed by the City of Orlando via the ParkMobile app. UnionWest al

ston Street (and an hourly lot near the Marriott) is managed by the City of Orlando via the ParkMobile app. UnionWest also has hourly visitor parking.
KEY FAQ POINTS
- Safety escorts: Available to students, faculty, staff, and visitors by calling (407) 823-5555.
- Permit needed downtown? Yes; a UCF parking permit works at both the Parramore Avenue and Amelia Street garages and is valid at UCF Downtown AND the main campus.
- Extra cost downtown? No — your UCF parking permit is valid at UCF Downtown.
- No car? Get to main campus via the direct UCF shuttle, driving, the LYNX bus, or SunRail.
- Ca

valid at UCF Downtown.
- No car? Get to main campus via the direct UCF shuttle, driving, the LYNX bus, or SunRail.
- Campus safety: UCF Downtown has a dedicated 24/7 police force in a sub-station on Amelia Street, with uniformed patrols, video surveillance, and blue-light phones.

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
UCF 2025-2026 Housing Agreement — Terms & Conditions
PARTIES & NATURE
A legally binding agreement between the UCF Board of Trustees ("University") and an admitted/enrolled student ("Student"). Submitting the agreement online (or delivering a signed hard copy) expresses the student's desire to occupy an assigned space; DHRL assigns the specific facility and room at its discretion. The agreement is effective immediately upon electronic signature/submission.
ARTICLE I — GENERAL
- Eligibility (1.4): Student must be admitted to the University and submit the agreement plus prepaid rent (unless waive

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text: - Eligibility (1.4): Student must be admitted to the University and submit the agreement plus prepaid rent (unless waived). While in residence, a student must be enrolled in and attending 12+ undergraduate or 6+ graduate credit hours in fall/spring, and/or 3+ credit hours in summer. If enrollment drops below these levels but is at least one credit hour, the student must apply in writing (Credit Hour Exemption Form) within 15 days. If a student drops to 0 credit hours, they must move out within 72 hours. The summer enrollment requirement is waived for continuing residents in Towers, NorthView,

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
st move out within 72 hours. The summer enrollment requirement is waived for continuing residents in Towers, NorthView, Rosen, and Greek communities.
- Term (1.5): Move-out must occur 24 hours after the student's last final exam or the academic-calendar move-out date, whichever is first. Early move-in is not guaranteed and may incur fees.
- Occupancy (1.6): Begins when the student signs for keys and ends when keys are returned per move-out procedures. A student may not designate another person to pick up/turn in keys.
- Non-transferable (1.7): Only the assigned student may occupy the space; no

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
ate another person to pick up/turn in keys.
- Non-transferable (1.7): Only the assigned student may occupy the space; no subletting, sharing, dividing, or transferring. Residents may not remove or add beds.
- Guest and visitor access (1.8): May be prohibited or restricted within the space or building. The student is responsible for guests' actions and damages. A student may not admit anyone they know has been evicted from housing or banned by a University authority.
- Authorized entry (1.9): Authorized UCF personnel may enter facilities in emergencies and for occupancy checks, lockouts, inspec

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
zed entry (1.9): Authorized UCF personnel may enter facilities in emergencies and for occupancy checks, lockouts, inspections (health/safety/maintenance/compliance), maintenance, and fire-code enforcement, or if they reasonably believe a violation/law-breaking is in progress. Personal property is not searched without consent except by law enforcement under law.
- Care and maintenance (1.10): Student must keep the space clean and sanitary, control pests, and not move assigned furniture. Florida's climate is conducive to mold/mildew; the student must properly ventilate and dehumidify per the Com

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
furniture. Florida's climate is conducive to mold/mildew; the student must properly ventilate and dehumidify per the Community Living Guide's Air Quality guidance.
- Space assignment (1.12): UCF assigns without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, and/or sexual orientation. DHRL does NOT guarantee a specific community, building, room, roommate, room type, or occupancy, and may consolidate or change assignments for order, health, safety, security, utilization, or nonpayment. Students who refuse consolidation may be charged additional rent.
- Accessibility (1.15): Accommodation

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
, or nonpayment. Students who refuse consolidation may be charged additional rent.
- Accessibility (1.15): Accommodation requests are submitted via the Housing Portal during the application process; DHRL may confer with Student Accessibility Services and/or Student Health Services. Information is kept confidential.
- Utilities (1.16): DHRL is not liable for utility interruptions; the University may assess additional charges with 30 days' written notice.
- Damage (1.17): Students are responsible for damage they cause; shared-area damage with no identified responsible person is the joint respons

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
nts are responsible for damage they cause; shared-area damage with no identified responsible person is the joint responsibility of all sharing the space.
- Adherence to policies (1.20): The DHRL Community Living Guide (CLG) and the UCF Golden Rule are incorporated into the agreement.
- Health and safety (1.21): Student acknowledges inherent risk of community living; UCF does not carry insurance for a student's illness/injury. Students must comply with pandemic-related policies (social distancing, symptom checking/testing, quarantine/isolation) and may be relocated; pandemic declarations may tr

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
s (social distancing, symptom checking/testing, quarantine/isolation) and may be relocated; pandemic declarations may trigger guest/visitor restrictions.
- Items left at departure (1.22): Belongings left after move-out (or 24+ hours after an unscheduled move-out) are deemed abandoned; DHRL may discard them, rekey locks, and charge the student.
ARTICLE II — RENT AND PAYMENT
- Rates (2.1): Posted at housing.ucf.edu/costs/rates/ and subject to change. Financial aid is applied to housing charges before any reimbursement; the agreement is not contingent on aid.
- Rental prepayment (2.2): $250.00 fo

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
housing charges before any reimbursement; the agreement is not contingent on aid.
- Rental prepayment (2.2): $250.00 for agreements beginning in spring or fall; $100.00 for summer. Prepayments are refunded only if DHRL cannot offer housing, for cancellations before set deadlines (Exhibit A), or at DHRL discretion.
- Payment (2.3): Rent is due by the dates on the Academic Calendar; DHRL does not send invoices — the student must monitor their account at my.ucf.edu. Students assigned after the due date get an automatic 14-day extension.
- Taxes (2.5): Agreements under six months + one day, offer

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
signed after the due date get an automatic 14-day extension.
- Taxes (2.5): Agreements under six months + one day, offered to non-full-time students, may be subject to ~12.5% Florida Sales/Use Tax and Orange County Tourist Development Tax.
- Default (2.6): Late fee up to $100 once per semester for overdue balances. DHRL may place a HOLD (blocking enrollment, registration, grades, diploma, transcripts), temporarily restrict residence access, and refer balances to collections (non-appealable).
- Florida Prepaid (2.7): FPCDP covers the cost of a standard air-conditioned double room only; the stud

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
non-appealable).
- Florida Prepaid (2.7): FPCDP covers the cost of a standard air-conditioned double room only; the student pays differences and any non-rental charges (e.g., lost keys, cleaning).
ARTICLE III — CANCELLATION AND TERMINATION
- Cancellation vs. termination (3.1): Cancellation can trigger additional rents/cancellation fees; termination is completion of or early release from the agreement and does not entitle DHRL to additional rents. Fees already charged remain due.
- Administrative cancellation (3.2): DHRL may cancel if a student is suspended/expelled, fails obligations (incl. ti

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
ue.
- Administrative cancellation (3.2): DHRL may cancel if a student is suspended/expelled, fails obligations (incl. timely payment or perfecting financial aid), is sanctioned for policy violations, or has unresolved matters from a prior agreement. DHRL may also relocate/terminate for order, health, discipline, disaster, or utilization.
- Cancellation fees (3.3-3.4): Charged when the agreement is cancelled; calculated by agreement duration, the date cancellation is received, and the date move-out completes (Exhibit A schedule). Due upon submission of the cancellation request.
- Procedure (3.5

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
and the date move-out completes (Exhibit A schedule). Due upon submission of the cancellation request.
- Procedure (3.5): All cancellation requests must be IN WRITING via the address in 1.19, the Housing Portal, or housing@ucf.edu.
- Effect of student cancellation (3.6): The student may remain through the end of the semester the notice was given (if still eligible). The student is always responsible for the greater of the pro-rata rent or the cancellation fee for that semester. Cancelling forfeits any current/prior-resident preference for future applications.
- Summer portion (3.7): A student

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
er. Cancelling forfeits any current/prior-resident preference for future applications.
- Summer portion (3.7): A student may not cancel only the third (summer) semester of a three-semester agreement during the fall without cancelling the spring portion too.
- Student terminations (3.8) — allowed with documentation for: Graduation; Academic Disqualification; Military Deployment (partial refund); Medical Withdrawal approved by Academic Services (partial refund; non-withdrawal medical conditions do not qualify); Internship/exchange/co-op that awards UCF credit, prevents residing for 3+ weeks, and

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
edical conditions do not qualify); Internship/exchange/co-op that awards UCF credit, prevents residing for 3+ weeks, and is 50+ miles from campus and the residence; Involuntary Withdrawal (partial refund); and other reasons at the discretion of the Assistant Vice President for Housing for the student's safety/well-being.
ARTICLE IV — SANCTIONS AND APPEALS
- Non-appealable bases (4.1): Financial inconvenience/changes in financial circumstance, and lack of classes located conveniently to the residence (including online or other-campus offerings), are NOT valid bases for termination.
- Sanctions

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
niently to the residence (including online or other-campus offerings), are NOT valid bases for termination.
- Sanctions (4.2): May include administrative warnings, monetary sanctions, referral to Student Conduct, and administrative cancellation with cancellation fees; multiple departments/boards may impose additional sanctions.
- Service fees (4.3): Charges are posted at housing.ucf.edu/costs/charges/.
ARTICLE V — MISCELLANEOUS
- Entire agreement (5.1): Written agreement supersedes oral understandings; amendments require a written instrument executed by DHRL.
- University interpretation contro

source_type: Official UCF PDF (legally binding housing agreement; revised 3/4/2025)
text:
rsedes oral understandings; amendments require a written instrument executed by DHRL.
- University interpretation controls (5.2).
- FERPA (5.3): DHRL may release personally identifiable information consistent with FERPA.
CONTACT (1.19): UCF Department of Housing & Residence Life, P.O. Box 163222, Orlando, FL 32816-3222; physical: 12851 Gemini Boulevard South, Orlando, FL 32816; Tel. (407) 823-4663; Fax (407) 823-3831; housing@ucf.edu.

source_type: Official UCF PDF (university-wide student conduct policies)
text:
The Golden Rule Student Handbook
WHAT IT IS
The Golden Rule is a compilation of policies and procedures from different university areas that defines the rights and responsibilities of UCF students. It is published online annually and may be revised at any time; revisions supersede previously published information. It is maintained by the Office of Student Conduct and Academic Integrity (SCAI) within the Division of Student Success and Well-Being (SSWB). Contact: 407-823-4638, scai@ucf.edu, http://scai.sswb.ucf.edu. It applies to all undergraduate, graduate, and professional students. The UCF C

source_type: Official UCF PDF (university-wide student conduct policies)
text:
scai@ucf.edu, http://scai.sswb.ucf.edu. It applies to all undergraduate, graduate, and professional students. The UCF Creed (Integrity, Scholarship, Community, Creativity, Excellence) frames the document. The Community Living Guide and Golden Rule are both incorporated into the Housing Agreement.
CONTENTS (regulations and sections)
- UCF-5.006 Student Rights and Responsibilities
- UCF-5.0065 Involuntary Withdrawal Procedures; Mandated Assessment
- UCF-5.007 Student Conduct and Academic Integrity; Scope; Records; Medical Emergencies (Alcohol & Drug)
- UCF-5.008 Code of Conduct
- UCF-5.009 Stud

source_type: Official UCF PDF (university-wide student conduct policies)
text:
nd Academic Integrity; Scope; Records; Medical Emergencies (Alcohol & Drug)
- UCF-5.008 Code of Conduct
- UCF-5.009 Student Conduct Review Process; Sanctions
- UCF-5.010 Student Conduct Appeals
- UCF-5.011 Student Group Responsibility; Organizational violations; Records; Medical Emergencies
- UCF-5.012 Organizational Code of Conduct
- UCF-5.013 Organization Conduct Review Process; Sanctions; Appeals
- UCF-5.015 Student Academic Misconduct Review Process
- UCF-5.016 Student Academic Appeals
- UCF-5.017 Appeals of Graduate Program Actions or Decisions
- Plus sections from: University Registrar;

source_type: Official UCF PDF (university-wide student conduct policies)
text:
ademic Appeals
- UCF-5.017 Appeals of Graduate Program Actions or Decisions
- Plus sections from: University Registrar; Student Health Services; Office of Student Involvement (Registered Student Organization Policy); Student Union; Office of Nondiscrimination & Accommodations Compliance (ONAC).
STUDENT RIGHTS (UCF-5.006) — summary
On enrollment, students are entitled (when exercised per University procedures and without disruption) to: participation in Student Government; membership in Registered Student Organizations; freedom of expression; freedom to hold public forums; freedom to hear, writ

source_type: Official UCF PDF (university-wide student conduct policies)
text:
bership in Registered Student Organizations; freedom of expression; freedom to hold public forums; freedom to hear, write, distribute, and act on a variety of thoughts and beliefs (with limits on solicitation and on distributing materials to captive audiences such as classrooms or residential units without permission); peaceful assembly; fair and impartial proceedings in disciplinary matters; confidentiality of student records; and accommodations for students with disabilities (via Student Accessibility Services).
STUDENT RESPONSIBILITIES
By enrolling, a student consents to University, Florida

source_type: Official UCF PDF (university-wide student conduct policies)
text:
s (via Student Accessibility Services).
STUDENT RESPONSIBILITIES
By enrolling, a student consents to University, Florida Board of Governors, and State of Florida policies/laws, and is responsible for knowing and abiding by University rules.
KEY DEFINITIONS (selected)
- Consent: an understandable exchange of affirmative words or actions indicating willingness to participate in mutually agreed sexual activity; must be informed and freely/actively given; cannot be obtained by force, threat, coercion, fear, intimidation, position of influence, or a person's incapacity; silence or lack of resistanc

source_type: Official UCF PDF (university-wide student conduct policies)
text:
rce, threat, coercion, fear, intimidation, position of influence, or a person's incapacity; silence or lack of resistance is not consent; can be withdrawn at any time. An incapacitated person (e.g., by alcohol/drugs, sleep, unconsciousness) cannot consent. Being impaired is no defense to a violation.
- Preponderance of Evidence: the "more probable than not" standard used in all disciplinary cases.
- Hold: a negative service indicator that can block registration changes, transcript access, and re-enrollment.
- Responsible Person: non-confidential university employees (incl. faculty, staff, RAs,

source_type: Official UCF PDF (university-wide student conduct policies)
text:
cript access, and re-enrollment.
- Responsible Person: non-confidential university employees (incl. faculty, staff, RAs, and graduate students with authority) who have reporting obligations.
- Student / University Premises / University Official / Title IX / Clery Act / VAWA / Complainant / Respondent: defined in the handbook (standard regulatory definitions).
SMOKING (UCF-5.006(4))
Students must uphold the smoke-free campus policy (http://smokefree.sswb.ucf.edu/). Smoking of any kind is prohibited in all facilities and on University premises.
MEDICAL EMERGENCIES / AMNESTY (UCF-5.006(5); detail

source_type: Official UCF PDF (university-wide student conduct policies)
text:
any kind is prohibited in all facilities and on University premises.
MEDICAL EMERGENCIES / AMNESTY (UCF-5.006(5); detailed in 5.007 and 5.011)
UCF strongly encourages students and Registered Student Organizations to call for medical assistance whenever someone experiences severe intoxication/impairment or serious injury after consuming alcohol and/or drugs. Recognizing students may hesitate due to potential law-enforcement or conduct involvement, the University urges calling emergency services; the primary goal is student safety. Specific amnesty procedures are in sections 5.007 (individuals)

source_type: Official UCF PDF (university-wide student conduct policies)
text:
emergency services; the primary goal is student safety. Specific amnesty procedures are in sections 5.007 (individuals) and 5.011 (organizations).
SEX-BASED MISCONDUCT & TITLE IX (UCF-5.006(6)-(7))
Non-Title IX sex-based misconduct is investigated by the Office of Nondiscrimination & Accommodations Compliance (ONAC) and adjudicated by SCAI using the preponderance standard. Both Complainant and Respondent have rights including: an advisor/support person (who may advise but not testify), remedial/protective measures, equal opportunity to present witnesses/information, impartial investigators/hea

source_type: Official UCF PDF (university-wide student conduct policies)
text:
testify), remedial/protective measures, equal opportunity to present witnesses/information, impartial investigators/hearing officers (with a chance to challenge impartiality), concurrent written notice of outcomes, the right to appeal, periodic status updates, and reasonable disability accommodations. Title IX Sexual Harassment is governed by UCF Policy 2-012.
INVOLUNTARY WITHDRAWAL (UCF-5.0065)
The University may involuntarily withdraw a student who poses a "Direct Threat" — a Significant Risk (high probability of harm, threats of harm, or substantial disruption) to the health/safety of othe

source_type: Official UCF PDF (university-wide student conduct policies)
text: — a Significant Risk (high probability of harm, threats of harm, or substantial disruption) to the health/safety of others, property, or University operations that cannot be mitigated by reasonable modifications. The procedure is applied non-discriminatorily and based on conduct, not on knowledge/belief of a disability. An Interim Involuntary Withdrawal may be imposed by the Dean of Students or designee based on reasonably available information; the student receives written notice and an opportunity to present information to the University Crisis Team within three business days. A Mandated Ass

source_type: Official UCF PDF (university-wide student conduct policies)
text:
otice and an opportunity to present information to the University Crisis Team within three business days. A Mandated Assessment (by a licensed professional) evaluates risk of harm to self or others.
CODE OF CONDUCT (UCF-5.008) — scope
Defines prohibited conduct (academic and non-academic), applies on and off campus as specified, and feeds the Student Conduct Review Process (5.009), sanctions, and appeals (5.010). Section 10 of the Code addresses controlled substances (referenced by the Community Living Guide).
